A. Derive a recurrence relation for the size (number of nodes) of the smallest AVL tree with height h. B. What is the size of the smallest AVL tree with height 9?

Match the pairs in the following: List 1: (a) Heap construction with linear probing (b) Constructing a Hashtable with linear probing (c) AVL tree construction (d) Digital trie construction List 2: (p) Theta(log_2(n)) (q)
The minimum number of interchanges needed to convert the array 89, 19, 40, 17, 12, 10, 2, 5, 7, 11, 6, 9, 70 into a max-heap is A. 0 B. 1 C. 2 D. 3

In a binary tree, a full node is defined to be a node with 2 children. Use induction on the height of the binary tree to prove that the number of full nodes plus one is equal to the number of leaves. Let F denote the num

A binary search tree is generated by inserting in order the following integers: 50, 15, 62, 5, 20, 58, 91, 3, 8, 37, 60, 24 The number of nodes in the left subtree and right subtree of the root respectively is A. (4, 7)

A binary search tree is used to locate the number 43. Which of the following probe sequences are possible and which are not? Explain. (a) 61 52 14 17 40 43 (b) 2 3 50 40 60 43 (c) 10 65 31 48 37 43 (d) 81 61 52 14 41 43

A binary search tree contains the values 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8. The tree is traversed in pre-order and the values are printed out. Which of the following sequences is a valid pre-order output? A. 5 3 1 2 4 7 8 6 B. 5 3
State whether the following statement is TRUE or FALSE: It is possible to construct a binary tree uniquely whose pre-order and post-order traversals are given. A. TRUE B. FALSE
State whether the following statement is TRUE or FALSE: If the number of leaves in a tree is not a power of 2, then the tree is not a binary tree. A. TRUE B. FALSE
Construct a binary tree whose preorder traversal is: K L N M P Q S T and inorder traversal is: N L K P M S Q T Draw the resulting binary tree and identify the root node.
